conditions: The closest 4 ft piling on the right is clear with texture, but the 11 ft piling and farther pilings are very hazy silhouettes and quickly disappear into green turbidity. This matches conditions where only the nearest piling is clearly visible.

The closest 4 ft piling on the right is clear with texture. The mid/right and farther pilings are only vague shadows with almost no detail, suggesting they are near or beyond the visibility limit. This matches conditions where only the nearest piling is truly clear.

Closest 4ft piling at right edge is clear with good texture. The 11ft piling is not distinctly visible; only a very hazy suggestion of a further piling toward the center-right, with no clear edges or texture. This matches conditions where only the nearest piling is clearly visible.

Closest 4 ft piling on the right is clear with good texture. The 11 ft piling is only barely discernible as a very faint, hazy silhouette and the 14 ft/30 ft pilings are not distinguishable. This matches the guideline where only the closest piling is clearly visible.

Closest 4ft piling at right edge is clear with good texture. The 11ft/14ft region (middle-right piling) is only barely discernible as a very hazy silhouette with almost no detail, and farther pilings are essentially lost in the haze. This corresponds to only the closest piling being truly clear.

Only the nearest 4 ft piling on the right edge is clearly visible; the 11 ft piling is not discernible and the background quickly fades into uniform green water with no other pilings or bottom visible. This corresponds to very poor conditions where only the closest structure is seen clearly.

Only the nearest piling edge is partially visible at the far right; all more distant pilings are completely obscured in uniform blue water with no discernible silhouettes, indicating very poor visibility under 5 ft.
